prompt: The method always prints 0 instead of the expected sum. What is the bug?
choices: static fields cannot be accessed from static methods
The local variable 'total' inside addUp shadows the static field — the method adds to the local copy, the static field stays 0
addUp must be called with System.out.println(addUp(...)) to print its return value
for-each loops cannot modify local variables inside a method
explanation: Declaring 'int total = 0' inside addUp creates a new local variable that shadows the static field with the same name. All additions go to the local variable; the static field is never touched. Fix: either use the return value (System.out.println(addUp(nums))), or remove the local declaration and use the static field directly (neither is great design — returning a value is cleaner).
